growth_records.medium: Canola oil minimal salt liquid medium
growth_records.cite: Salihu Ibrahim et al. Biosurfactant Production and Growth Kinetics Studies of the Waste Canola Oil-Degrading Bacterium Rhodococcus erythropolis AQ5-07 from Antarctica. Molecules (2020). DOI:10.3390/molecules25173878. [PMC7503493]
growth_records.snippet: The calculated values for the Haldane model constants maximum specific growth rate (qmax), half saturation constant (Ks) and growth inhibition constant (Ki) were 0.142 h-1, 7.743% (v/v) and 0.399% (v/v), respectively.
growth_records.notes: qmax = 0.142 h-1 is the maximum specific growth rate from the Haldane substrate-inhibition model (best-fit among 7 models) fitted to experimental OD600 growth-rate-vs-canola-oil-concentration data (1-7% v/v) measured over 84 h; growth_method=calculated (model-derived from measured OD slopes). doubling_time_h computed as td = ln2/qmax = 0.693/0.142 = 4.88 h. Kinetic constants: Ks = 7.743% (v/v), Ki = 0.399% (v/v). NOTE internal inconsistency: the displayed Haldane equation (Eq. 1) lists a leading coefficient of 0.0669 whereas the abstract, Results text, and Table 1 state qmax = 0.142 h-1; the reported qmax value (0.142 h-1) was used. Growth-kinetics cultivation done at 10 C, 150 rpm, pH 7.5, in minimal-salt canola-oil medium; strain isolated from King George Island, maritime Antarctica. NO substrate uptake rate could be quantified: authors state 'The determination of the canola oil degradation kinetic variables was not possible because of the liquid nature of the oil.' No product secretion rates, no MFA fluxes, and no biomass/substrate yields reported; substrate concentrations are volumetric (% v/v canola oil), so no mmol/gDW/h uptake is derivable.

phenotypes.base_medium: Substrate-utilisation assay; base medium per the original source
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 10866 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/10866
